commit.message:
perf: reduce allocations in token stream formatting

# PR Glass Cockpit

Make review boring. Make truth cheap.

## 💡 Summary
Added explicit capacity hints to String allocations in `tokmd-format`
rendering functions. This avoids repeated memory reallocations when
rendering reports for repositories with many files or languages.

## 🎯 Why (perf bottleneck)
Functions like `render_lang_md`, `render_module_md`, and
`render_diff_md` were initializing with `String::new()` and then
repeatedly extending the string in loops based on row data. This causes
intermediate memory reallocations which hurts runtime performance and
CPU utilization on larger inputs.

## 📊 Proof (before/after)
Performance was measured using `cargo bench -p tokmd-format` with tables
of 1000 rows (`render_lang_tsv_1000`, `render_module_tsv_1000`,
`render_module_md_1000`):

- `render_module_tsv_1000` improved from ~174 µs to ~161 µs (-14.5% to
-1.2%)
- `render_lang_tsv_1000` improved from ~185 µs to ~171 µs (-9.6% to
-7.3%)
- `render_module_md_1000` improved from ~190 µs to ~172 µs (-10.4% to
-9.5%)

## 🧭 Options considered
### Option A (recommended)
- What it is: Use `String::with_capacity` based on a heuristic of rows
and column character count.
- Why it fits this repo: Maintains absolute output determinism, is
highly isolated (SRP), and doesn't change API signatures.
- Trade-offs: Requires a heuristic size which might over-allocate
slightly, but prevents expensive `O(N)` reallocations.

### Option B
- What it is: Refactor formatting to pass a `&mut dyn Write` and stream
directly to output.
- When to choose it instead: If peak memory usage is the critical issue
and allocating the full formatted string is unacceptable.
- Trade-offs: Would require changing API signatures across the
`tokmd-format` crate.

## ✅ Decision
Option A was chosen as it delivers an easy-to-trust performance win
without altering public APIs or structure.

## 🧱 Changes made (SRP)
- `crates/tokmd-format/src/lib.rs`
- Replaced `String::new()` with `String::with_capacity()` using length
heuristics in Markdown and TSV renderers.

## 🧪 Verification receipts
- `cargo bench -p tokmd-format` (verified improvements)
- `cargo build`
- `CI=true cargo test --verbose` (Pass)
- `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` (Pass)
- `cargo fmt -- --check` (Pass)

## 🧭 Telemetry
- Change shape: Shallow string pre-allocation
- Blast radius (API / IO / format stability / concurrency): Purely
internal implementation detail. No API or format changes.
- Risk class + why: Extremely low. Determinism verified by snapshot
tests.
- Rollback: Revert to `String::new()`
- Merge-confidence gates: standard gates.

## 🗂️ .jules updates
- Initialized `.jules/policy/scheduled_tasks.json` and basic `.jules`
runbooks for Bolt.
- Created `20260303T132005Z.json` envelope, `2026-03-03.md` run log, and
updated `ledger.json` in `.jules/bolt/`.

---

[9006619675249841418](https://jules.google.com/task/9006619675249841418)
started by @EffortlessSteven*

Implement incremental key facts accumulation with genre-aware extraction (#10)

* refactor: reorganize prompts into Application.Prompts namespace

- Create Application/Prompts/ folder with Narrator.cs and KeyFacts.cs classes
- Move key facts extraction logic from KeyFactExtractor into GameEngine as private methods
- Update GameEngine.ApplyTurnAsync to extract key facts after summary refresh
- Rename PromptBuilderTests to NarratorTests, KeyFactExtractorTests to GameEngineKeyFactsTests
- Delete old files: PromptBuilder.cs, KeyFactExtractor.cs, and old test files
- Update PROJECT_BRIEF.md with new architecture
- All 13 tests pass, zero build warnings

* feat: implement JSON-based key facts extraction

- Add KeyFactsJson property to GameState for structured game state storage
- Update KeyFacts prompts to request JSON output with predefined schema
- Include genre-agnostic schema with categories: setting, characters, objects, narrative, flags
- Replace string-based GetKeyFactsAsync/AddKeyFactsAsync with JSON versions
- Update Narrator.BuildTurnPrompt to pass KeyFactsJson to AI for context
- Update GameEngineTests.cs with JSON format expectations
- All 3 tests pass, zero build warnings
- Adheres to coding standards and .editorconfig

* Address warnings

* refactor: separate schemas from prompts for key facts extraction

- Create src/Application/Schemas/Agnostic.cs with parameterized schema
- Update KeyFacts.cs: replace SystemPrompt property with BuildSystemPrompt(jsonSchema) method
- Update GameEngine.cs to import Application.Schemas and call BuildSystemPrompt(Agnostic.Schema)
- Update GameEngineTests.cs to verify KeyFactsJson contains extracted state
- Enables future genre-specific schemas (Fantasy, SciFi, etc.) without modifying prompts
- All 9 tests pass (4 Application + 1 Domain + 4 Infrastructure)
- Zero build warnings
- Adheres to coding standards and .editorconfig

* style: format whitespace changes (no functional changes)

* feat: add genre detection and genre-specific key fact schemas

Detect story genre from the player's theme at premise generation time and
use a genre-tailored JSON schema when extracting key facts, so the AI
captures richer, more relevant structured state per genre (Fantasy, Horror,
Mystery, SciFi, Western). Falls back to Agnostic on any failure or unknown
genre. Registers GenreDetector in the IoC container and updates all tests.

* feat: implement incremental key facts accumulation

Instead of re-extracting key facts from the full story log each turn,
use the existing KeyFactsJson + latest turn only. Fresh extraction runs
when KeyFactsJson is empty; subsequent turns use BuildUpdatePrompt to
merge new facts into the existing JSON. Invalid AI responses fall back
to the previous KeyFactsJson rather than storing garbage.

- Extract genre detection prompts into Application.Prompts.GenreDetector

Wire forceResetOnClose, noZeroReads, noReleaseInterface, ignoreHeaderErrors,
brokenSendObjectPropList, brokenSetObjectPropList, skipCloseSession,
propListOverridesObjectInfo, and samsungPartialObjectBoundaryBug into the
transport layer (LibUSBTransport, MTPUSBLink) and protocol layer (DeviceActor).

Transport-level flags are propagated via SwiftMTPConfig; protocol-level flags
are read from DevicePolicy at the actor level. Includes 13 integration tests
covering all 9 flags using TrackingLink mock.

fix(db): set uor=0 for deprecated care_team layout fields (#10849)

Fresh installs show deprecated `care_team_provider`, `care_team_status`,
and `care_team_facility` DEM layout fields as visible (`uor=1`), while
the 7.0.3→7.0.4 upgrade script correctly hides them (`uor=0`). This
inconsistency means fresh installs and upgraded installs behave
differently for fields that have been superseded by the
`care_team_member` table. Additionally, users who installed fresh on
8.0.0 will have these fields set incorrectly and need a remediation path
via the upgrade script.

## Changes

- **`sql/database.sql`**: Set `uor=0` on the three deprecated
`care_team_*` `layout_options` INSERT statements, aligning fresh
installs with the state produced by the upgrade path. Incremented the
`v_database` marker to 535.
- **`version.php`**: Incremented `$v_database` from 534 to 535 to stay
in sync with the `database.sql` marker.
- **`sql/8_0_0-to-8_0_1_upgrade.sql`**: Added a `#IfRow3D`-guarded
UPDATE to set `uor=0` for the three deprecated `care_team_*` fields,
ensuring users who installed fresh on 8.0.0 (and thus have `uor=1`) are
corrected when upgrading to 8.0.1.

```sql
-- Before (database.sql)
VALUES ('DEM', 'care_team_provider', ..., 1, ...)
VALUES ('DEM', 'care_team_status',   ..., 1, ...)
VALUES ('DEM', 'care_team_facility', ..., 1, ...)

-- After (database.sql)
VALUES ('DEM', 'care_team_provider', ..., 0, ...)
VALUES ('DEM', 'care_team_status',   ..., 0, ...)
VALUES ('DEM', 'care_team_facility', ..., 0, ...)

-- Added to 8_0_0-to-8_0_1_upgrade.sql
UPDATE `layout_options` SET `uor` = 0
  WHERE `form_id` = 'DEM'
  AND `field_id` IN ('care_team_facility', 'care_team_provider', 'care_team_status')
  AND `uor` = 1;
```

commit.message:
transport: implement IOUSBHost file transfer (getObject/sendObject) (#491)

Upgrade executeStreamingCommand in IOUSBHostLink to support large file
transfers through chunked bulk I/O:

- Data-in: parse PTP data container header to determine total payload
  length, then loop reading bulk-in chunks until complete. Handles the
  0xFFFFFFFF sentinel for files >4GB and early response containers.

- Data-out: send PTP data container header first, then stream payload
  in 64KB chunks from the callback instead of accumulating in memory.
  Uses 0xFFFFFFFF sentinel for containers exceeding UInt32 capacity.

These changes enable getObject, sendObject, getPartialObject, and
sendPartialObject to work through the existing ProtoTransfer layer
which dispatches via executeStreamingCommand.

Add comprehensive PTP container framing tests covering:
- Data-in header parsing and multi-chunk reassembly
- Large file sentinel value handling (0xFFFFFFFF)
- Data-out container encoding
- GetObject/SendObjectInfo/SendObject command framing
- GetPartialObject/GetPartialObject64/SendPartialObject param encoding
- Response container parsing with error codes

Fix config propagation for EnvironmentCredential and WorkloadIdentityCredential properties (#56721)

* Fix config propagation for EnvironmentCredential and WorkloadIdentityCredential properties

Add support for setting all EnvironmentCredential and WorkloadIdentityCredential
properties via IConfigurationSection, with environment variables as fallback.

Properties added to config pipeline:
- ClientId, ClientSecret, ClientCertificatePath, ClientCertificatePassword,
  SendCertificateChain, Username, Password (EnvironmentCredential)
- TokenFilePath (WorkloadIdentityCredential)

Changes:
- DefaultAzureCredentialOptions: add internal properties, read from config,
  propagate in Clone()
- DefaultAzureCredentialFactory: propagate properties in CreateEnvironmentCredential()
  and CreateWorkloadIdentityCredential()
- Add 14 new tests validating config-wins-over-env-var and fallback behavior

Fixes #56715

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add config-only TokenFilePath test for WorkloadIdentityCredential

Add TokenFilePath_FromConfigOnly_CreatesCredential test to verify that
WorkloadIdentityCredential can be created purely from config when the
AZURE_FEDERATED_TOKEN_FILE env var is not set.

Add Homebrew tap automation to release workflow

Add update-tap job that runs after each release to:
- Compute SHA256 checksums for all platform archives
- Generate a Homebrew formula (macOS arm64, Linux amd64/arm64)
- Push the formula to gammelby/homebrew-tap

The formula installs both tmux-remote (CLI client) and
tmux-remote-agent binaries, bundles the Nabto Client SDK
shared library, and fixes rpaths for the Homebrew layout.

Requires TAP_GITHUB_TOKEN secret with write access to
gammelby/homebrew-tap.

commit.message:
Forms: Add embed code panel to form editor (#47238)

* Forms: Add embed code panel to form editor

Add a document inspector panel to the form editor that allows users
to copy the embed code for embedding forms in posts and pages.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add CopyCodeRow and embed code rows

Introduce a reusable CopyCodeRow component (with styles) to display a code snippet and a copy button with confirmation. Replace the old inline copy button in the embed panel with PluginPostStatusInfo rows that show both the embed HTML and the shortcode, each using CopyCodeRow. Files added: copy-code-row.tsx, copy-code-row.scss; modified: embed-code-panel.tsx to use CopyCodeRow, to generate embedCode and shortcode, and to switch from PluginDocumentSettingPanel to PluginPostStatusInfo.

* fix import order

* Forms: Address PR review feedback for embed code panel

- Fix plugin name to follow jetpack-form-* convention
- Fix inaccurate docblock and comment text
- Add shortcode copy button alongside embed code
- Replace per-button margin with flex gap layout
- Add unit tests for EmbedCodePanel component
- Fix changelog trailing period

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix prettier formatting in embed code panel test

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Replace tooltip with visible label in CopyCodeRow

Add a label prop that renders above the code snippet, replacing the
tooltip wrapper for better discoverability.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Select all text on click in CopyCodeRow

Clicking the code text now selects its full content, making it easier
to copy manually. Adds cursor: text and user-select: all CSS, plus
keyboard accessibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Increase spacing between embed code rows

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix Popover placement for RTL locales

Use top-end/top-start placement based on text direction so the
"Copied!" confirmation appears correctly in RTL languages.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix docblock to clarify post-type scoping

The component itself doesn't check post type; registration logic in
form-editor/index.tsx handles that.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address Copilot review feedback for CopyCodeRow

- Use ownerDocument.createRange() instead of global document
- Add preventDefault/stopPropagation in keyboard handler
- Add aria-readonly to the textbox span
- Make copy button label describe the action (e.g. "Copy embed code")

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Move Copy %s translation into variable

Extract the translatable string "Copy %s" into a buttonLabel constant and add a translator comment. The Button now uses sprintf(buttonLabel, label.toLowerCase()) instead of calling __() inline, improving clarity and ensuring a single translation entry.

* update test

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* minor fixes
